label: Finger sprain with negative x-ray and no tendon or neurovascular red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit a finger sprain.
found: Your clinician diagnosed a finger sprain. Your x-ray did not show a fracture, and we did not find tendon injury, infection, neurovascular compromise, or another emergency problem today.
home: Rest the finger from painful gripping or impact.
Use splinting, buddy taping, ice, elevation, or activity changes only as instructed.
Move the finger only in the way your clinician said is safe.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Avoid medicines your clinician told you to avoid.
return: New numbness, weakness, color change, severe pain, or inability to move the finger.
Fever, spreading redness, warmth, pus, or red streaks.
Worsening swelling, new deformity, or pain that is not improving.
follow: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, orthopedics, or hand surgery if pain, swelling, or motion is not improving.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of finger sprain/strain.
X-ray performed and negative, with intact neurovascular exam and no tendon injury, open wound, infection, high-risk mechanism, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Fracture or dislocation concern, tendon rupture, open wound, infection, flexor tenosynovitis concern, neurovascular compromise, severe pain, inability to move the finger, high-energy trauma, or crush injury.
Pregnancy/pediatric pathway or specialist-directed hand/orthopedic plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports sprain/strain framing, protection/rest/elevation concepts, gradual return to activity, and escalation for worsening pain, swelling, numbness, color change, fever, inability to move, or inability to walk. Autonomous Phase 2001-2100 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed finger sprain/strain with negative x-ray, intact neurovascular exam, and no tendon injury, infection, high-risk mechanism, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of finger sprain/strain
no tendon injury, infection, open wound, high-risk mechanism, or specialist-directed plan

label: Toe sprain with negative x-ray and no open wound or diabetic-foot risk
summary: Your symptoms fit a toe sprain.
found: Your clinician diagnosed a toe sprain. Your x-ray did not show a fracture, and we did not find an open wound, infection, diabetic-foot risk, neurovascular compromise, or another emergency problem today.
home: Rest from painful walking or pressure.
Use buddy taping, a shoe, ice, elevation, or activity changes only as instructed.
Protect the toe from repeat injury while it heals.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Avoid medicines your clinician told you to avoid.
return: New numbness, color change, severe pain, or worsening inability to walk.
Spreading redness, warmth, pus, red streaks, fever, or an open wound.
New deformity, worsening swelling, or symptoms not improving.
follow: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, podiatry, or orthopedics if pain, walking, or swelling is not improving.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of toe sprain/strain.
X-ray performed and negative, with intact neurovascular exam and no open wound, infection, diabetic-foot/PVD/neuropathy risk, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Fracture or dislocation concern, open wound, nail-bed injury requiring separate pathway, infection, neurovascular compromise, severe pain, inability to bear weight, high-energy trauma, or crush injury.
Diabetic-foot risk, neuropathy, PVD, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed podiatry/orthopedic plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports sprain/strain framing, protection/rest/elevation concepts, gradual return to activity, and escalation for worsening pain, swelling, numbness, color change, fever, inability to move, or inability to walk. Autonomous Phase 2001-2100 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed toe sprain/strain with negative x-ray, intact neurovascular exam, and no open wound, infection, diabetic-foot/PVD/neuropathy risk, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of toe sprain/strain
no open wound, infection, diabetic-foot risk, or specialist-directed plan

label: Uncomplicated rib contusion without fracture or cardiopulmonary red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit a rib or chest wall bruise.
found: Your clinician diagnosed an uncomplicated rib or chest wall contusion. We did not find fracture concern, lung injury, heart or blood-clot concern, or another emergency problem today.
home: Rest from activities that worsen the pain.
Use ice, heat, or gentle movement only as instructed.
Take slow deep breaths as your clinician instructed so you do not avoid breathing because of pain.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Avoid medicines your clinician told you to avoid.
return: Shortness of breath, coughing blood, fainting, pressure-like chest pain, or pain with exertion.
Fever, worsening cough, or feeling very ill.
Severe worsening pain, new trauma, or symptoms not improving.
follow: Follow up with primary care or urgent care if pain or breathing comfort is not improving.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated rib/chest wall contusion.
No fracture concern, pneumothorax/pulmonary injury concern, cardiac/PE concern, hypoxia, hemoptysis, syncope, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Fracture/dislocation concern, high-energy trauma, pneumothorax or pulmonary injury concern, hypoxia, hemoptysis, cardiac/PE concern, syncope, fever/systemic illness, severe pain, or unstable vitals.
Pregnancy/pediatric pathway or specialist-directed trauma/orthopedic plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports bruise/contusion framing, local care concepts, and escalation when bruising is severe, unusual, or associated with concerning symptoms. Autonomous Phase 2001-2100 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed rib/chest wall contusion without fracture concern, cardiopulmonary red flags, high-risk trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated rib/chest wall contusion
no fracture concern, cardiopulmonary red flags, high-risk trauma, or specialist-directed plan documented

label: Shoulder overuse tendinitis without rupture or neurovascular red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit shoulder tendinitis or an overuse shoulder strain.
found: Your clinician diagnosed uncomplicated shoulder tendinitis or overuse strain. We did not find tendon rupture, fracture, dislocation, infection, neurovascular compromise, or another emergency problem today.
home: Rest from lifting or overhead activity that worsens pain.
Use ice, heat, a sling, stretching, or activity changes only as instructed.
Return to activity gradually as pain and motion improve.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Avoid medicines your clinician told you to avoid.
return: New weakness, numbness, color change, severe pain, or inability to use the arm.
Fever, red hot joint, spreading redness, or feeling very ill.
New trauma, deformity, or symptoms not improving.
follow: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, physical therapy, or orthopedics if pain, motion, or strength is not improving.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated shoulder tendinitis/overuse strain.
No tendon rupture, fracture/dislocation concern, septic joint/infection concern, neurovascular compromise, high-energy trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Tendon rupture, fracture/dislocation concern, acute traumatic mechanism with instability, septic joint/infection concern, red hot joint, open wound, neurovascular compromise, severe pain, or inability to use the arm.
Pregnancy/pediatric pathway or specialist-directed orthopedic plan.
must_not_miss: Referred cardiac pain when presentation is not clearly shoulder overuse.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports tendinitis and sprain/strain framing, rest/activity modification, clinician-directed symptom care, and escalation for worsening pain, swelling, numbness, color change, fever, inability to move, or inability to walk. Autonomous Phase 2001-2100 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated shoulder tendinitis/overuse strain without rupture, fracture/dislocation, infection, neurovascular compromise, high-risk trauma, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated shoulder tendinitis/overuse strain
no rupture, fracture/dislocation, infection, neurovascular compromise, high-risk trauma, or specialist-directed plan documented

label: Uncomplicated wrist tendinitis without fracture or neurovascular red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit wrist tendinitis or an overuse wrist strain.
found: Your clinician diagnosed uncomplicated wrist tendinitis or overuse strain. We did not find fracture, tendon rupture, infection, neurovascular compromise, or another emergency problem today.
home: Rest from gripping or repetitive activity that worsens symptoms.
Use a splint, ice, heat, stretching, or activity changes only as instructed.
Return to activity gradually as symptoms improve.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Avoid medicines your clinician told you to avoid.
return: New numbness, weakness, color change, severe pain, or inability to use the hand.
Fever, spreading redness, warmth, pus, or red streaks.
New trauma, deformity, worsening swelling, or symptoms not improving.
follow: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, physical therapy, orthopedics, or hand surgery if pain, motion, or function is not improving.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated wrist tendinitis/overuse strain.
No fracture/dislocation concern, tendon rupture, flexor tenosynovitis concern, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Fracture/dislocation concern, FOOSH/scaphoid risk, tendon rupture, flexor tenosynovitis concern, open wound, infection, neurovascular compromise, severe pain, inability to use the hand, high-energy trauma, or crush injury.
Pregnancy/pediatric pathway or specialist-directed hand/orthopedic plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports tendinitis and sprain/strain framing, rest/activity modification, clinician-directed symptom care, and escalation for worsening pain, swelling, numbness, color change, fever, inability to move, or inability to walk. Autonomous Phase 2001-2100 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated wrist tendinitis/overuse strain without fracture/dislocation concern, tendon rupture, infection, neurovascular compromise, high-risk mechanism, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated wrist tendinitis/overuse strain
no fracture/dislocation, rupture, infection, neurovascular compromise, high-risk mechanism, or specialist-directed plan documented
